ori: Isolated from the venom of the Neotropical wasp Polybia dimorpha
strn: Mycobacterium abscessus subsp. massiliense isolates GO 01, GO 06, GO 07, GO 08, GO 13, and GO 18, CRM0020 (Mycobacterium abscessus subsp. massiliense), and ATCC19977 (Mycobacterium abscessus subsp. abscessus)
cline: Peritoneal macrophages, J774 macrophages cells, human red blood cells
inhi: Treatment of infected macrophages with 7.6 Î¼g/mL of Polydim-I decreased approximately 50% of the bacterial load
ctox: Non-cytotoxic, 10% cytotoxicity on J774 cells at concentrations above 121.6 Î¼g/mL (10%) and concentrations up to 121.6 Î¼g/mL presented less than 2.5% of hemolytic activity
ivmod: 6 to 8 weeks old BALB/c and IFN-Î³KO (Knockout) female mice
dose: 2 mg/kg/mLW shows 90% reduction in bacterial load

ori: Produced by a genetically distinct Nonomuraea species, strain MJM5123.
cline: Vero cells (ATCC CRL-1586), J774.1 macrophage cell line, Caco-2 cell monolayers
inhi: 0.12 Î¼M, ecumicin  reduced M. tuberculosis viabilities in J774 macrophages by 2 x log10
ivmod: Female BALB/c mice, male CD-1 mice
dose: 20 mg/kg causes reductions in M. tuberculosis lung
mech: Ecumicin markedly enhanced the ATPase activity of wild-type (WT) ClpC1 but prevented activation of proteolysis by ClpC1. so toxic proteins which are normally degraded by the ClpC1P1P2 complex, accumulate in the presence of ecumicin
comth: Ecumicin encapsulated in micelles distributes to mouse lung tissue
